title: Date Calculator (add/subtract days, or difference between dates)
shortDescription: Add or subtract years/months/days from a date, or find the precise difference between two dates including business days.
description: Two modes: 'add or subtract' to compute a future or past date, or 'between' to find the difference in years, months, days, weeks, and business days.
metaDescription: Free date calculator. Add or subtract years/months/days, or find difference between two dates with business-day count. Calendar-aware, leap years included.

sections.heading: What it computes
paragraphs: Add/subtract mode: takes a date, adds or subtracts a number of years, months, and days, and gives you the resulting calendar date. Years and months use calendar arithmetic (so adding 1 month to Jan 31 gives Feb 28 or Feb 29 depending on leap year, not Mar 3). Days are exact — adding 30 days always advances exactly 30 calendar days.

Between mode: takes two dates and gives the difference in years/months/days plus totals in days, weeks, months, and business days (Monday through Friday, no holidays). The Y/M/D form uses the same borrow logic as our age calculator — a difference is e.g. '2 years, 5 months, 3 days', not a decimal year.

sections.heading: Why business days don't include holidays
paragraphs: We count Monday through Friday as business days and exclude Saturday and Sunday. Public holidays vary by country, region, and year, so we don't subtract them automatically. For US business-day work, subtract roughly 10 federal holidays per year from the result; for UK, about 8 bank holidays; for Japan, about 16.

If holidays matter for your calculation (deadline tracking, contract negotiations), do the calendar count here, then check your country's holiday list manually. We may add country-specific holiday subtraction in a future version.

sections.heading: Common use cases
paragraphs: Project deadlines: 'this is due 90 days after kickoff'. Add 90 days to the start date.
Contract terms: count days between contract start and end. Match against your contract language for billing.
Travel planning: how many business days between leaving and returning? Useful for out-of-office calendars and expense reports.
Personal milestones: anniversaries, sobriety counts, time since a major life event.

howTo.name: Pick a mode
howTo.text: Add/subtract for moving from one date, or between for finding a difference.

howTo.name: Add the offset or end date
howTo.text: Years/months/days for add mode, or end date for between mode.

howTo.name: Read the result
howTo.text: Resulting date or full difference breakdown.

faq.q: What happens if I add 1 month to Jan 31?
faq.a: You get Feb 28 (or Feb 29 in a leap year). Calendar arithmetic clamps to the last day of the target month when the source day doesn't exist.

faq.q: Are holidays subtracted from business days?
faq.a: No — only weekends. Subtract the country-specific holidays manually based on your jurisdiction's calendar.

faq.q: Does it handle negative date arithmetic?
faq.a: Yes via the 'subtract' mode. Internally, subtraction is just adding negative deltas. The 'difference between' mode always returns positive values regardless of order.

faq.q: What time zone does this use?
faq.a: Your browser's local time zone. Dates are interpreted as midnight local. Cross-time-zone calculations may need adjustment.

faq.q: Can I add hours and minutes?
faq.a: Not yet — date granularity only. Use a separate timestamp tool for hour-level math.

faq.q: What's the largest difference it handles?
faq.a: Hundreds of years comfortably. JavaScript's Date supports ±100 million days from 1970, so this is the practical limit.

faq.q: Why does it count both endpoints differently?
faq.a: Total days is the count of nights between the two dates. Two consecutive days have a difference of 1 day. Business day count walks through each calendar day in between.

faq.q: Does the data leave my browser?
